name: Silk Hero Reveal
logic: GSAP timeline pins hero, scrubs reveal on scroll. Silk-smooth because GSAP runs on rAF and skips layout thrash.
constraint: Pinning breaks if user has smooth-scroll browser extensions. ~50kb gzipped.
mitigation: Detect extensions via window.scrollY vs scroll event; fall back to fade-in.

name: GPU-Composited Parallax
logic: Parallax via transform: translate3d() forces GPU layer. No layout, no paint, only composite.
constraint: Too many GPU layers (>30) exhausts VRAM on mobile.
mitigation: Cap concurrent parallax elements to 8; use will-change: transform sparingly.

name: Kinetic Typography
logic: SplitText breaks heading into chars; stagger reveals each char with spring physics.
constraint: SplitText is a premium GSAP plugin (paid Club).
mitigation: Use the open-source split-type library instead; lose MorphSVG but keep char splitting.
use_case: Hero headlines, section dividers, agency sites

name: Layout Morph
logic: Flip records element positions, you change layout, Flip animates the difference. Filter reflows become smooth.
constraint: Flip needs a state change to animate from; SSR-first apps must hydrate before Flip can record.
mitigation: Gate Flip behind useEffect; show skeleton during hydration.
use_case: Filterable grids, kanban boards, image galleries

name: SVG Path Morph
logic: MorphSVG tweens between two SVG paths by point-matching. Logo-to-logo transitions, icon-to-icon.
constraint: MorphSVG is Club GreenSock only (paid). Paths must have similar point counts for clean morphs.
mitigation: Use flubber.js (open-source alternative) for cross-platform path morphing.

name: Spring Physics Drag
logic: Framer Motion's springs respond to drag velocity — swipe fast = fly far. Native-feeling on touch.
constraint: Cascading springs across many elements settle unpredictably.
mitigation: Cap concurrent springs; use a single config object for all springs in a group.

name: AnimatePresence Route Exit
logic: AnimatePresence wraps route children; exit animations play before unmount. Smooth page transitions.
constraint: App Router doesn't natively support AnimatePresence — needs a template.tsx wrapper.
mitigation: Use the template.tsx pattern; or downgrade to Pages Router for animation-critical routes.

name: Staggered Card Grid
logic: Container variant staggers child reveal by 50ms each. Reads as 'premium loading' instead of 'data arrived'.
constraint: Stagger across >20 items feels slow.
mitigation: Cap stagger at first 12 items; rest appear immediately after.

name: Magnetic Cursor
logic: Cursor position drives a useMotionValue; buttons within 80px translate toward cursor. Tactile, premium.
constraint: Touch devices have no cursor — the effect is invisible. Battery drain on always-running rAF.
mitigation: Detect coarse-pointer via matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)'); disable on touch.

name: Scroll-Linked Hue Shift
logic: ScrollTrigger updates --hue CSS variable; entire palette shifts as user scrolls. One timeline, global effect.
constraint: Hue shift on text reduces contrast at certain scroll positions.
mitigation: Lock text hue; only shift background/accent hues.

name: Native SPA Transitions
logic: Browser snapshots old DOM, morphs to new on route change — zero JS, hardware-composited.
constraint: Browser support gaps (Firefox <2024); less granular than JS timelines.
mitigation: @supports (view-transition-name) guard + progressive enhancement.

name: Native C++ Timeline
logic: element.animate() runs on the browser's native compositor — same perf as CSS, but with JS play/pause/seek/reverse.
constraint: No spring physics out of the box; easing must be cubic-bezier.
mitigation: Pair with Popmotion for spring; WAAPI for the actual render.

name: Framework-Agnostic Physics
logic: Popmotion is the pure-math core behind Framer Motion — springs, decay, pointers — no Virtual DOM weight.
constraint: No React bindings; you wire it yourself.
mitigation: Wrap in a 30-line usePopMotion hook; or use Motion One (Popmotion's DOM wrapper).

name: Tactile Drag Physics
logic: Spring physics (mass/tension/friction) respond to swipe velocity — no jarring cuts when interrupted mid-gesture.
constraint: Cascading springs across many elements → unpredictable settling.
mitigation: Cap concurrent springs; use useSprings with a single config object.

name: Staggered Micro-Interactions
logic: Anime.js v4 is 6kb gzipped, framework-agnostic, and supports staggered timelines out of the box.
constraint: No native scroll-timeline engine — manual event rigging for parallax.
mitigation: Add GSAP ScrollTrigger ONLY for scroll; Anime for everything else.

name: Route-Exit Animations
logic: Framer Motion's AnimatePresence handles route exits; Tailwind handles the visual system.
constraint: App Router needs template.tsx wrapper; AnimatePresence adds ~30kb.
mitigation: Import motion/m only (tree-shake AnimatePresence if unused).
use_case: Premium SaaS, marketing sites with personality

name: Native Scroll Animations
logic: Browser-native scroll-linked animations — animation-timeline: scroll() — zero JS.
constraint: Chrome/Safari only as of 2025; Firefox behind flag.
mitigation: @supports guard + IntersectionObserver fallback.
use_case: Parallax, scroll progress bars, pinned sections

name: Card-to-Modal Morph
logic: view-transition-name on card and modal; browser morphs between them on state change.
constraint: Only one element can have a given name per snapshot — collisions break the morph.
mitigation: Dynamically assign names; clear them after transitionend.
use_case: Image galleries, card-to-detail flows, master-detail UIs

name: Reveal-on-Scroll, Zero Deps
logic: IO fires once → element.animate() plays reveal. ~400 bytes of JS, native compositor perf.
constraint: No stagger built-in — you sequence manually.
mitigation: Use a data-stagger-index attribute and delay = index * 50ms.
use_case: Blogs, docs, content sites where bundle size matters

name: Utility-First Velocity
logic: Declarative string variants replace motion hooks; Tailwind handles layout.
constraint: No canvas/WebGL/procedural pipeline — shallow ceiling.
mitigation: Use for marketing pages; graduate to GSAP when you hit the ceiling.

name: 3D Hero Scene
logic: R3F renders a 3D scene declaratively; camera orbits on scroll or pointer. Memorable first impression.
constraint: Three.js is ~600kb; mobile GPUs struggle with >50k triangles.
mitigation: Lazy-load below fold; use instancing for repeated geometry; cap pixel ratio at 1.5 on mobile.
use_case: Product launches, immersive marketing, game/film sites

name: Shader Material Hero
logic: Custom fragment shader renders a generative background (gradient noise, fluid sim) at 60fps on GPU.
constraint: GLSL is a separate skill — most frontend devs can't write shaders. Debug tooling is weak.
mitigation: Use shader-park or lygia (shader libraries) for premade patterns; customize uniforms only.
use_case: Premium brand sites, AI product launches

name: Particle Field
logic: 10k-50k GPU-instanced points drift in 3D space; camera moves through them. Depth without geometry cost.
constraint: Transparent particles thrash the GPU on mobile if depth-sorting is on.
mitigation: Disable depthWrite on particle material; use additive blending.

name: 3D Product Configurator
logic: Load GLTF product model; user orbits, swaps materials, sees real-time updates. Sells better than 2D photos.
constraint: GLTF files are 2-20MB; mobile data plans suffer.
mitigation: Use Draco compression; serve LOD variants based on connection speed.

name: Scroll-Driven 3D Camera
logic: Scroll position drives camera Z through a 3D scene. Story unfolds spatially as user scrolls.
constraint: Camera path must be baked; dynamic paths cause jitter.
mitigation: Precompute camera curve with CatmullRom; lerp scroll input.

name: AR Try-On
logic: WebXR session places 3D objects in the user's real space via camera. Try glasses, furniture, shoes in AR.
constraint: iOS Safari requires Quick Look (USDZ) not WebXR. Android Chrome supports WebXR.
mitigation: Detect platform; serve USDZ on iOS, WebXR on Android, 3D viewer fallback on desktop.

name: Volumetric Video
logic: Play back captured 3D video (point cloud or mesh sequence) in Three.js. Hologram feel.
constraint: Volumetric capture is expensive; files are huge (100MB+ per minute).
mitigation: Stream via tiles; only load visible frustum. Use NERF or Gaussian Splatting for compression.

name: Interactive 3D Map
logic: Mapbox provides geo data; Three.js renders extruded 3D buildings on top. Tilt + rotate for spatial feel.
constraint: Two WebGL contexts (Mapbox + Three) conflict on some GPUs.
mitigation: Use Mapbox's built-in Three.js layer (custom layer); single context.
use_case: Real estate, urban planning, logistics dashboards

name: 3D Data Viz
logic: 3D bar charts, network graphs, particle scatter plots. Spatial depth makes dense data legible.
constraint: 3D charts can mislead (perspective distortion). Accessibility tools can't read 3D.
mitigation: Pair 3D view with a 2D data table; let users toggle. Provide aria-labels per data point.

name: Hand-Tracking Interaction
logic: Quest browser / Vision Pro tracks hand joints; pinch to grab 3D objects. No controller needed.
constraint: Hand tracking is Quest+/Vision Pro only; Chrome desktop doesn't support it.
mitigation: Use mouse + keyboard fallback; gate hand-tracking behind feature detection.
